dailyJudgementSnippet: Google Gemini had the best HN vibe today: Gemma itself got some skepticism, but the Gemini Robotics thread still generated more optimistic capability talk than any other tracked provider received [E6][E7].
winnerExplanation: Google Gemini wins on balance because it had one clearly upbeat thread around robotics capability [E7], which outweighed a separate but milder skeptical Gemma-on-iPhone discussion [E6]. OpenAI's signal was mixed-to-negative overall, with a neutral-to-mixed product launch thread and a direct security criticism [E2][E1]. Anthropic leaned negative because Claude was discussed mainly in legal/privacy-risk terms [E4], and Microsoft Copilot's clearest mention was a blunt unfavorable comparison in the Excel thread [E3].

openai.judgementSnippet: Mixed to slightly negative: ChatGPT for Excel sparked interest, but comments were split between excitement and slowness/privacy worries, and another thread included a direct knock on OpenAI's security claims [E2][E1].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Slightly negative: Claude appeared mainly in legal/privacy discussion about privilege, with only a modest mixed-use endorsement in the OpenClaw thread [E4][E5].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Slightly positive overall: Gemma on iPhone drew skepticism about practicality, but Gemini Robotics produced the day's strongest forward-looking enthusiasm around capability [E6][E7].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: Negative: Copilot's main direct mention was an unfavorable comparison saying it mostly opens a side panel and that Copilot for Excel is useless [E3].

evidence.summary: In the cybersecurity thread, a commenter said an AI tool bypassed ChatGPT/Turnstile protections in hours and concluded that OpenAI's security boasts were worthless.
ChatGPT for Excel drew curiosity and some favorable comparison against Microsoft, but comments also warned about slowness and workbook data being shared with OpenAI.
A commenter said Microsoft's Copilot button 'doesn't do much more than open a chat side panel' and that Copilot for Excel is useless.
The legal-privilege thread centered on Claude chats; commenters worried Claude's terms and use patterns could undermine attorney-client protection.
In the OpenClaw Ask HN thread, one user said Claude handled part of a quoting workflow well but not perfectly, while another mentioned tuning away from expensive Opus/Sonnet-heavy usage.
Gemma-on-iPhone comments were skeptical, calling it a flashy demo, noting battery-draining GPU inference, and saying the embedded model was not smart enough for more ambitious coding tasks.
Gemini Robotics comments were notably forward-looking about spatial reasoning and planning, including a user report that Gemini mostly drove a small rover successfully.
